evals.name: claude-headless-implementation
evals.prompt: Use Claude Code to implement the approved API pagination changes in this repo. Keep it headless and summarize the changed files.
evals.expected_output: The skill should route the task to Claude Code CLI, prefer `claude --print`, and avoid adding elevated permission flags unless explicitly allowed.
expectations: The response uses or recommends `claude --print` for the headless implementation run.
The response runs from the intended repository or workspace.
The response does not invent a model or effort value.
The response does not silently add `--permission-mode bypassPermissions` or `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.

evals.name: claude-review-diff
evals.prompt: Use Claude Code to review the current git diff for bugs, compatibility issues, and hidden blast radius. Do not edit files.
evals.expected_output: The skill should use a headless Claude Code review prompt that is review-only and findings-first.
expectations: The response uses or recommends `claude --print` for the review run.
The review prompt treats the diff as primary scope.
The review prompt requires bounded impact tracing through callers, references, consumers, contracts, compatibility assumptions, and immediate upstream/downstream paths.
The review prompt says not to edit files or start build/test work.

evals.name: claude-model-effort-pass-through
evals.prompt: Run Claude Code with sonnet and high effort to investigate this regression and patch it if needed.
evals.expected_output: The skill should pass through the requested model and effort without inventing unrelated defaults.
expectations: The response includes `--model sonnet`.
The response includes `--effort high`.
The response uses `--print` if the run is headless or automated.
The response does not add unrelated permission or tool flags unless justified by the prompt.

evals.name: claude-read-only-explanation
evals.prompt: Use Claude Code to explain how the caching layer works in this repo. It should be read-only and must not edit files.
evals.expected_output: The skill should use a headless, no-edit Claude Code run and may restrict tools or permissions.
expectations: The response encodes the read-only/no-edit constraint in the prompt.
The response uses `claude --print` for the headless run.
The response may use allowed/disallowed tool flags to tighten the run.
The response does not use Cursor `--mode ask` or `--mode plan`.

evals.name: generic-subagent-no-claude
evals.prompt: Please use a subagent to inspect the auth tests and report back. I do not care which built-in helper you use.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not trigger because the user asked for a generic or built-in subagent, not Claude Code CLI.
expectations: The response does not introduce `claude --print` or other Claude Code CLI commands.
The response treats the request as generic delegation rather than Claude Code selection.
The response may ask which external executor to use if external execution is required.

evals.name: wrong-executor-codex
evals.prompt: Run Codex on this repo to implement the UI copy update. Keep it non-interactive.
evals.expected_output: The Claude Code skill should not activate because the user selected Codex CLI, not Claude Code.
expectations: The response does not use `claude` commands.
The response recognizes that Codex CLI is the selected executor.
The response does not substitute Claude Code for the named executor.

evals.name: ordinary-claude-chat-no-cli
evals.prompt: In this Claude conversation, explain the tradeoffs of headless coding agents. Do not run the Claude Code CLI.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not turn ordinary Claude chat context into a Claude Code CLI launch.
expectations: The response does not launch or recommend `claude --print`.
The response respects the instruction not to run Claude Code CLI.
The response treats the task as normal explanation.

evals.name: unspecified-external-agent-no-claude
evals.prompt: Delegate this migration risk audit to an external coding agent. Pick whatever is convenient.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not assume Claude Code merely because the user asked for an unspecified external coding agent.
expectations: The response does not default to `claude` without an explicit selection.
The response asks for executor choice or uses a separate orchestration policy outside this skill.
The response does not present Claude Code-specific flags as if Claude Code had already been chosen.
